title: Testing with Generator
content:
# Testing with Generator In the Kelvin SDK there is a `kelvin-publisher` test tool to help you ingest data to the inputs and debug the outputs to test the performance of your Kelvin SmartApp™. It is a CLI utility for exercising your application during development. It is not imported into your code and is used only as a standalone testing tool. You must have installed the `kelvin-python-sdk[publisher]` library for this feature to work. The Kelvin SDK generator publisher tool is designed to allow testers to ingest messages from another Python script for testing purposes. The general command to start the **Test Generator** tool is; ```bash title="Starting Test Tool" linenums="1" kelvin-publisher generator --entrypoint : ``` You will then run your Kelvin SmartApp™ as normal in a second terminal and the generator app will feed the simulation data into it. ## Generator Tool Overview The generator publisher tool supports a variety of options to customize its behavior. Below is a list of the available options: | Test Parameters | Required | Description | Note | |-----------------------------|-------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------| | `--config TEXT` | **optional** | Path to the `app.yaml` | **Default**: Current directory | | `--entrypoint | **required** | Path to the entrypoint (setuptools style) of the generator python class. It can be path a .py file or path to a module. Eg: mygenerator.py:MyGenerator | | | `--asset-count INTEGER` | **optional** | Number of Assets (test-asset-N) | **Default**: 1 | ## Video Tutorial You can watch this **Event Callback** video tutorial which will show you how to setup a Kelvin SmartApp™ and create a Generator Test Python script to test the app all on you local machine with the Kelvin Test Tools. In the following chapters after the video tutorial you can see and copy all the scripts shown in the video tutorial. The Generator Tool section starts at [minute 2:23](https://youtu.be/L_ZWngLzd0M?feature=shared&t=143). ## Generator Python Script This is a local Python script you use to create the data and send it to the Kelvin SmartApp™ using the command line `kelvin-publisher` tool. Click on the :fontawesome-solid-circle-plus:{ .code-descriptions } for details about the code ```py title="main.py" linenums="1" import asyncio # (1)! from typing import AsyncGenerator import random import string import json from kelvin.krn import KRNAssetDataStream # (2)! from kelvin.publisher import DataGenerator, MessageData # (3)! from kelvin.message import Number, Message, CustomAction # $ kelvin-publisher generator --help # # Usage: kelvin-publisher generator [OPTIONS] # # Publishes data generated by a custom generator class # # Options: # --config TEXT Path to the app config file [default: app.yaml; # required] # --entrypoint TEXT Path to the entrypoint (setuptools style) of the # generator python class. It can be path a .py file or # path to a module. Eg: mygenerator.py:MyGenerator # [required] # --asset-count INTEGER Number of test assets from 'test-asset-1' to 'test- # asset-N' # --help Show this message and exit. # # # i.e.: # # $ kelvin-publisher generator --entrypoint generator.py:MyNumberGenerator class MyNumberGenerator(DataGenerator): # (4)! def __init__(self): print("Hello from MyNumberGenerator") async def run(self) -> AsyncGenerator[MessageData, None]: print("Running MyNumberGenerator") for i in range(10): yield MessageData( resource=KRNAssetDataStream("", "sa-rw-number"), value=i, ) await asyncio.sleep(1) class MyBooleanGenerator: # (6)! def __init__(self): print("Hello from MyBooleanGenerator") async def run(self) -> AsyncGenerator[MessageData, None]: print("Running MyBooleanGenerator") while True: i=bool(random.getrandbits(1)) yield MessageData( resource=KRNAssetDataStream("", "sa-rw-bool"), timestamp=None, value=i, ) await asyncio.sleep(1) class MyStringGenerator: # (7)! def __init__(self): print("Hello from MyStringGenerator") async def run(self) -> AsyncGenerator[MessageData, None]: print("Running MyStringGenerator") while True: i=''.join(random.choices(string.ascii_letters,k=7)) # initializing size of string yield MessageData( resource=KRNAssetDataStream("", "sa-rw-text"), timestamp=None, value=str(i), ) await asyncio.sleep(1) class MyObjectGenerator: # (8)! def __init__(self): print("Hello from MyObjectGenerator") async def run(self) -> AsyncGenerator[MessageData, None]: print("Running MyObjectGenerator") file = open('object.json') # returns JSON object as # a dictionary data = json.load(file) while True: yield MessageData( resource=KRNAssetDataStream("", "sa-object"), timestamp=None, value=data, ) await asyncio.sleep(1) class CCGenerator: # (9)! def __init__(self): print("Hello from CCGenerator") async def run(self) -> AsyncGenerator[MessageData, None]: print("Running CCGenerator") for i in range(20, 30): yield MessageData( resource=KRNAssetDataStream("", "sa-cc-number"), timestamp=None, value=i ) await asyncio.sleep(1) class CustomActionGenerator(DataGenerator): def __init__(self) -> None: print("Hello from MyGenerator") async def run(self) -> AsyncGenerator[Message, None]: print("Running MyGenerator") for i in range(10): yield CustomAction( resource=KRNAsset("test-asset-1"), type="email", payload={ "to": "operations@example.com", "subject": "Recommendation to reduce speed", "body": "This is the email body", } ) await asyncio.sleep(1) ``` 1. The `asyncio` module is used to ensure the program is running in asynchronous mode. This allows code, especially that which typically waits such as I/O-bound operations, to effectively execute in parallel thereby making the most efficient use of processor time. 2. The `KRNAssetDataStream` class is used to define the Asset / Data Stream pair names into the Kelvin format. KRN stands for [Kelvin Resource Name](../../../../overview/concepts/krn.md). 3. The `DataGenerator` and `MessageData` are two classes used for declaring the Generator and creating the Message to be sent as the test data. 4. The `MyNumberGenerator` and `MyOtherNumberGenerator` class creates a number from 1 to 10 and sends it as test number data once per second for 10 seconds. 5. The `MyNumberGenerator` and `MyOtherNumberGenerator` class creates a number from 1 to 10 and sends it as test number data once per second for 10 seconds. 6. The `MyBooleanGenerator` class randomly choose either `True` or `False` and sends it as test boolean data once per second. 7. The `MyStringGenerator` class creates a 7 letter word with random letters and sends it as test string data once per second. 8. The `MyObjectGenerator` class load data from a file (it can be generated by the Python script to) and sends it as test object data once per second. See below for a sample of the object data as a dynacard, though in reality it can be anything. 9. The `CCGenerator` class creates a control change for an Asset / Data Stream pair and sends it as control change object data once per second for 10 seconds. 10. The `CustomActionGenerator` class creates a series of Custom Actions type `email` as a Custom Action object data once per second. ## Test your SmartApp 1. **Requirements** Normally you will use the `kelvin-publisher` on Kelvin SmartApps™, you will be working with a complete complimentary set of files created by `kelvin app create`. As we are only interesting in showing the abilities of the **Generator Tool** we will just create two files, `main.py` and `app.yaml` for this demo purpose. You can learn more about how to [create a Kelvin SmartApp™ in the "Development" ⟹ "Create a SmartApp" page](../create.md). **main.py** In order to show how to use the generator, we will use it with the example event callback app in the menu Development ⟹ Consume.
[Event Callbacks Example Script](../consume/event-callbacks.md){ .md-button .md-button--primary }
If you are not interested in the code, you can simply copy and paste this script into a file called `main.py`. ```py title="main.py" linenums="1" import asyncio from kelvin.application import KelvinApp from kelvin.message.typing import AssetDataMessage async def on_control_change(msg: AssetDataMessage): # Get Asset and Value asset = msg.resource.asset value = msg.payload print("Received Control Message: ", msg) print(f"Asset: {asset}, Value: {value}") async def main() -> None: # Initialize the SmartApp app = KelvinApp() # Define the function to call when event callback is triggered app.on_control_change = on_control_change # Connect the SmartApp with the Kelvin Platform await app.connect() # Force the Application to run forever while True: await asyncio.sleep(1) if __name__ == "__main__": asyncio.run(main()) ``` **app.yaml** For the app.yaml file, simply copy this and paste into a file called `app.yaml`. ```yaml title="app.yaml" linenums="1" spec_version: 5.0.0 type: application name: sa-e2e-consumer-app title: sa-e2e consumer App Generator description: Consumer App version: 1.0.0 flags: enable_runtime_update: configuration: false resources: false parameters: true resource_properties: true data_streams: inputs: - data_type: number name: sa-rw-number - data_type: string name: sa-rw-text - data_type: boolean name: sa-rw-bool - data_type: object name: sa-object control_changes: - data_type: number name: sa-cc-number ``` 2. **Run the Generator Test Tool:** To test by sending numbers to your Kelvin SmartApp™ ```bash title="Starting Test Tool with Number Generator" linenums="1" kelvin-publisher generator --entrypoint generator.py:MyNumberGenerator ``` To test by sending control changes to your Kelvin SmartApp™ ```bash title="Starting Test Tool with Control Change Generator" linenums="1" kelvin-publisher generator --entrypoint generator.py:MyCCGenerator ``` and you should see the following output which includes any print statement from the generator Python script and the Kelvin Publisher class itself: ```bash title="Starting Test Tool Output" linenums="1" Hello from Publisher started. Listening on 0.0.0.0:49167 ``` 3. **Open a NEW terminal window and run the Kelvin SmartApp™**: ```bash title="Running Kelvin SmartApp™ Python Example" linenums="1" python3 main.py ``` and you should see the following output: ```bash title="Kelvin SmartApp™ Python Example Output" linenums="1" Received Data Message: id=UUID('720f9e8d-1dd2-4054-afb4-5d579b31e998') type=KMessageTypeData('data', 'pt=number') trace_id=None source=None timestamp=datetime.datetime(2024, 10, 28, 21, 30, 40, 440062, tzinfo=datetime.timezone(datetime.timedelta(seconds=25200), '+07')) resource=KRNAssetDataStream(asset='test-asset-1', data_stream='sa-rw-number') payload=0.0 Asset: test-asset-1, Value: 0.0 Received Data Message: id=UUID('ca147a01-2f88-4ba6-8d93-5bb186967574') type=KMessageTypeData('data', 'pt=number') trace_id=None source=None timestamp=datetime.datetime(2024, 10, 28, 21, 30, 41, 441811, tzinfo=datetime.timezone(datetime.timedelta(seconds=25200), '+07')) resource=KRNAssetDataStream(asset='test-asset-1', data_stream='sa-rw-number') payload=1.0 Asset: test-asset-1, Value: 1.0 (...) ``` 4. **Go to the Generator Tool terminal and check the logs:** ```bash title="Test Tool Output" linenums="1" (...) Connected Running MyNumberGenerator ```
summary: In the Kelvin SDK there is a kelvin-publisher test tool to help you ingest data to the inputs and debug the outputs to test the performance of your Kelvin SmartApp™. It is a CLI utility for exercising your application during development. It is not...

code_examples.code:
import asyncio # (1)!
from typing import AsyncGenerator
import random
import string
import json
from kelvin.krn import KRNAssetDataStream # (2)!
from kelvin.publisher import DataGenerator, MessageData # (3)!
from kelvin.message import Number, Message, CustomAction

# $ kelvin-publisher generator --help
#
# Usage: kelvin-publisher generator [OPTIONS]
#
#   Publishes data generated by a custom generator class
#
# Options:
#   --config TEXT          Path to the app config file  [default: app.yaml;
#                          required]
#   --entrypoint TEXT      Path to the entrypoint (setuptools style) of the
#                          generator python class. It can be path a .py file or
#                          path to a module. Eg: mygenerator.py:MyGenerator
#                          [required]
#   --asset-count INTEGER  Number of test assets from 'test-asset-1' to 'test-
#                          asset-N'
#   --help                 Show this message and exit.
#
#
# i.e.:
#
# $ kelvin-publisher generator --entrypoint generator.py:MyNumberGenerator

class MyNumberGenerator(DataGenerator): # (4)!
    def __init__(self):
        print("Hello from MyNumberGenerator")

    async def run(self) -> AsyncGenerator[MessageData, None]:
        print("Running MyNumberGenerator")
        for i in range(10):
            yield MessageData(
                resource=KRNAssetDataStream("", "sa-rw-number"),
                value=i,
            )
            await asyncio.sleep(1)

class MyBooleanGenerator: # (6)!
    def __init__(self):
        print("Hello from MyBooleanGenerator")

    async def run(self) -> AsyncGenerator[MessageData, None]:
        print("Running MyBooleanGenerator")
        while True:
            i=bool(random.getrandbits(1))
            yield MessageData(
                resource=KRNAssetDataStream("", "sa-rw-bool"),
                timestamp=None,
                value=i,
            )
            await asyncio.sleep(1)

class MyStringGenerator: # (7)!
    def __init__(self):
        print("Hello from MyStringGenerator")

    async def run(self) -> AsyncGenerator[MessageData, None]:
        print("Running MyStringGenerator")
        while True:
            i=''.join(random.choices(string.ascii_letters,k=7)) # initializing size of string
            yield MessageData(
                resource=KRNAssetDataStream("", "sa-rw-text"),
                timestamp=None,
                value=str(i),
            )
            await asyncio.sleep(1)

class MyObjectGenerator: # (8)!
    def __init__(self):
        print("Hello from MyObjectGenerator")

    async def run(self) -> AsyncGenerator[MessageData, None]:
        print("Running MyObjectGenerator")
        file = open('object.json')
        # returns JSON object as
        # a dictionary
        data = json.load(file)
        while True:
            yield MessageData(
                resource=KRNAssetDataStream("", "sa-object"),
                timestamp=None,
                value=data,
            )
            await asyncio.sleep(1)

class CCGenerator: # (9)!
    def __init__(self):
        print("Hello from CCGenerator")

    async def run(self) -> AsyncGenerator[MessageData, None]:
        print("Running CCGenerator")
        for i in range(20, 30):
            yield MessageData(
                resource=KRNAssetDataStream("", "sa-cc-number"),
                timestamp=None,
                value=i
            )
            await asyncio.sleep(1)

class CustomActionGenerator(DataGenerator):
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        print("Hello from MyGenerator")

    async def run(self) -> AsyncGenerator[Message, None]:
        print("Running MyGenerator")

        for i in range(10):
            yield CustomAction(
                    resource=KRNAsset("test-asset-1"),
                    type="email",
                    payload={
                    "to": "operations@example.com",
                    "subject": "Recommendation to reduce speed",
                    "body": "This is the email body",
                }
            )
            await asyncio.sleep(1)
code_examples.title: main.py

code_examples.code:
import asyncio

from kelvin.application import KelvinApp
from kelvin.message.typing import AssetDataMessage

async def on_control_change(msg: AssetDataMessage):
    # Get Asset and Value
    asset = msg.resource.asset
    value = msg.payload

    print("Received Control Message: ", msg)
    print(f"Asset: {asset}, Value: {value}")

async def main() -> None:

    # Initialize the SmartApp
    app = KelvinApp()

    # Define the function to call when event callback is triggered
    app.on_control_change = on_control_change

    # Connect the SmartApp with the Kelvin Platform
    await app.connect()

    # Force the Application to run forever
    while True:
        await asyncio.sleep(1)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())
code_examples.title: main.py

code_examples.code:
spec_version: 5.0.0
type: application

name: sa-e2e-consumer-app
title: sa-e2e consumer App Generator
description: Consumer App
version: 1.0.0

flags:
  enable_runtime_update:
    configuration: false
    resources: false
    parameters: true
    resource_properties: true

data_streams:
  inputs:
    - data_type: number
      name: sa-rw-number
    - data_type: string
      name: sa-rw-text
    - data_type: boolean
      name: sa-rw-bool
    - data_type: object
      name: sa-object

control_changes:
    - data_type: number
      name: sa-cc-number

steps.instruction: The `asyncio` module is used to ensure the program is running in asynchronous mode. This allows code, especially that which typically waits such as I/O-bound operations, to effectively execute in parallel thereby making the most efficient use of processor time.
The `KRNAssetDataStream` class is used to define the Asset / Data Stream pair names into the Kelvin format. KRN stands for Kelvin Resource Name.
The `DataGenerator` and `MessageData` are two classes used for declaring the Generator and creating the Message to be sent as the test data.
The `MyNumberGenerator` and `MyOtherNumberGenerator` class creates a number from 1 to 10 and sends it as test number data once per second for 10 seconds.
The `MyNumberGenerator` and `MyOtherNumberGenerator` class creates a number from 1 to 10 and sends it as test number data once per second for 10 seconds.
The `MyBooleanGenerator` class randomly choose either `True` or `False` and sends it as test boolean data once per second.
The `MyStringGenerator` class creates a 7 letter word with random letters and sends it as test string data once per second.

The `MyObjectGenerator` class load data from a file (it can be generated by the Python script to) and sends it as test object data once per second. See below for a sample of the object data as a dynacard, though in reality it can be anything.
The `CCGenerator` class creates a control change for an Asset / Data Stream pair and sends it as control change object data once per second for 10 seconds.
The `CustomActionGenerator` class creates a series of Custom Actions type `email` as a Custom Action object data once per second.
